steps.label: Board overview
steps.caption: Your team's Linear board shows all issues organized by workflow status — a live snapshot of what everyone is working on.

steps.label: Open quick issue creator
steps.caption: Press C from anywhere in Linear to open the quick issue creator instantly, without leaving your current view.

steps.label: Enter issue title
steps.caption: Type a clear, searchable title. Linear uses it across notifications, filters, and search — precision here saves time later.

steps.label: Add a description
steps.caption: The description field accepts rich text, images, and mentions — add context that makes the issue self-explanatory.

steps.label: Set status
steps.caption: The status picker maps to your team's workflow. Issues start as Todo and move forward as work progresses.

steps.label: Set priority
steps.caption: Priority tells your team what to tackle first. You can filter and sort by priority across any Linear view.

steps.label: High priority selected
steps.caption: High priority flags this issue for prompt attention — it surfaces in priority-filtered views and daily digests.

steps.label: Assign to a teammate
steps.caption: Assigning the issue sends a notification and makes it appear in the assignee's My Issues view immediately.

steps.label: Link to a cycle
steps.caption: Linking the issue to a cycle adds it to your sprint. Cycles give Linear its time-boxed, goal-driven structure.

steps.label: Create the issue
steps.caption: Click Create Issue to save it instantly. Linear assigns a unique ID for tracking in commits, PRs, and Slack.

steps.label: Issue appears on the board
steps.caption: The new issue appears in the Todo column right away — no refresh needed. The toast confirms the ID for quick reference.

steps.label: View issue details
steps.caption: Click any issue to open the full detail view, where you can add comments, sub-issues, estimates, and attachments.
